title: Gate Maintenance, Upgrades, Metrics, and Capstone · Gate Forensics
summary: Operate a reusable repository template without letting gates rot or overwhelm contributors. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reusable quality-gated repository template and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Measure defect escape, false positives, duration, flakiness, suppression age, and developer friction.
Schedule compatibility-tested tool and runtime upgrades with rollback paths.
Raise standards gradually with changed-code gates and expiring debt records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: gates maintenance. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Quality Metrics trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure defect escape, false positives, duration, flakiness, suppression age, and developer friction.

content.text: For Quality Metrics, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-capstone-defense-103ioe: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-capstone-defense-103ioe:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Capstone Defense precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Capstone Defense applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each gate by risk, ownership, feedback time, evidence, and maintenance plan.
